dsl_rule.translation_notes: Mapped 'panic volatility spike >2.5 standard deviations' to hvol_20 > 2.5 (historical volatility in decimal scale). Mapped 'price moved >7% contrary to thesis in <7 days' to returns condition (7% = 0.07). Used rsi_14 extremes as confirmation signal proxy: RSI < 30 for bearish thesis entry (price down), RSI > 70 for bullish thesis entry (price up). Direction is CONTEXT_DEPENDENT as signal applies to both long and short based on trader's thesis. Stop loss set at 8% drawdown per parameters.

backtest_rule.reason: Mapped 'panic volatility spike >2.5 standard deviations' to hvol_20 > 2.5 (historical volatility in decimal scale). Mapped 'price moved >7% contrary to thesis in <7 days' to returns condition (7% = 0.07). Used rsi_14 extremes as confirmation signal proxy: RSI < 30 for bearish thesis entry (price down), RSI > 70 for bullish thesis entry (price up). Direction is CONTEXT_DEPENDENT as signal applies to both long and short based on trader's thesis. Stop loss set at 8% drawdown per parameters.

source_rule_text: When market panic or hysteria creates extreme price moves, a contrarian trader with a foundational bearish/bullish thesis should maintain the position through the chaos, as panic-driven moves are unsustainable and will reverse.
